content: Run a metagenomics pipeline with quality-filtered, host-depleted paired-end reads (and trimmed versions for abundance estimation). It assembles contigs, evaluates assembly quality, predicts coding sequences, then branches on a single yes/no flag: either create a full non-redundant gene catalog with clustering, functional and taxonomic annotation, per-sample abundance, and resistance gene detection, or focus on the resistome by identifying resistance genes first, retaining only genes from contigs that carry them, annotating that subset, clustering the resistance genes, and quantifying their abundance across samples. Outputs include the catalog sequences, annotations, abundance tables, resistance reports, assembly metrics, and a summary linking genes/resistance genes to taxonomy, function, and abundance. Use meta_genomic_R1.fastqsanger.gz, meta_genomic_R2.fastqsanger.gz. Use concise task language and assume domain familiarity. Prefer this guidance when available: metagenomics pipeline. Return structured outputs for functional_annotation_tool, workflow_step_count, assembly_tool.
